methods.description: Fourier infrared spectroscopy (FTIR) analysis was conducted to identify active biomolecules in the aqueous extract of T. domingensis pollen grains, which are responsible for the reduction and stabilization of silver nitrate into silver nanoparticles (AgNPs).

steps.description: FTIR spectra of the plant extract and nanoparticle mixture were recorded.
steps.searchable_summary: record FTIR spectra of plant extract and nanoparticle mixture
inputs: aqueous extract of T. domingensis pollen grains

steps.description: Analyze FTIR spectra to identify peaks and their corresponding biomolecules.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze FTIR spectra to identify biomolecule peaks

steps.description: Compare FTIR peaks of plant extract and qrdAgNPs to identify shifts indicating biomolecule involvement in AgNP stabilization.
steps.searchable_summary: compare FTIR peaks to identify shifts and biomolecule involvement

methods.description: X-ray diffraction (XRD) analysis was performed to confirm the crystalline nature of qrdAgNPs synthesized by the aqueous extract of T. domingensis pollen grains.

steps.description: Perform XRD analysis to obtain diffraction peaks of qrdAgNPs.
steps.searchable_summary: perform XRD analysis to obtain diffraction peaks

steps.description: Analyze XRD diffraction peaks to determine crystalline planes and confirm the crystalline nature of AgNPs.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze XRD peaks to determine crystalline planes

steps.description: Identify additional peaks in XRD patterns resulting from organic compounds in the plant extract.
steps.searchable_summary: identify additional XRD peaks from organic compounds
